question_text: Explain why a gas can be compressed more easily than a liquid.
correct_answer: Gas particles are far apart with large spaces between them, so they can be pushed closer together. Liquid particles are already close together with small spaces, so there is less room to compress them further.
explanation: This question requires students to link particle spacing to observable properties. In gases, particles are far apart, allowing more room for compression. In liquids, particles are already tightly packed, so compression is difficult. This demonstrates understanding of how particle arrangement explains macroscopic behaviour.
